findings_details.title: makeSessionsMock uses `as any` to suppress type errors on an incomplete mock
findings_details.body: makeSessionsMock() and makeSessionsWithInstance() both cast their return value `as any` to suppress TypeScript errors (lines 22 and 41). The sessions mock lacks several fields required by ReturnType<typeof useTerminalSessions> (e.g. knownActiveTerminals, attachExisting, needsAttention, switchTo). Tests that call useTerminalPanel with this mock exercise real code paths that read those fields (useAutoReattach reads knownActiveTerminals and attachExisting), but because the fields are silently undefined the effect is a silent no-op rather than a verified pass. The `as any` is hiding a contract violation. Fix: define a proper typed partial: `Partial<ReturnType<typeof useTerminalSessions>>` satisfied by the mock, and cast only at the hook call site as `sessions as ReturnType<typeof useTerminalSessions>`. Then add knownActiveTerminals and attachExisting fields so useAutoReattach can be exercised.

findings_details.title: Debounce test asserts `<= 2` fits instead of exactly 1
findings_details.body: The refit-coalescing test at line 191 asserts `expect(fit.mock.calls.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(2)`. The stated goal in the comment is '5 rapid ResizeObserver events — debounced into ONE fit call'. The assertion permits 2 calls, weakening the guarantee. If the debounce is broken (e.g. the clearTimeout is missing), all 5 triggers could fire individual timeouts and the test would still pass as long as two or fewer fired before the assertion. Fix: tighten the assertion to `expect(fit.mock.calls.length).toBe(1)` to match the stated intent. The test already uses fake timers and advances exactly 100 ms, so one call is the correct invariant.

findings_details.title: Long inline className string in SidebarPanel makes the mobile-visibility logic hard to audit
findings_details.body: Line 49 concatenates a 120+ character className string inline: `className={\`min-h-0 md:min-w-[300px] md:w-[33%] w-full md:border-r border-paper-200 md:flex flex-col ${show ? 'flex' : 'hidden md:flex'}\`}`. The mobile show/hide condition (`flex` vs `hidden md:flex`) is buried at the end of the string, making it hard to verify that mobile-only styles are correctly guarded. Fix: extract to a named variable `const visibilityClass = show ? 'flex' : 'hidden md:flex'` and use it in the className, keeping the static classes as a separate constant. This is a readability issue, not a correctness bug, but the buried condition makes future mobile-layout changes error-prone.

findings_details.title: Mixed inline style and Tailwind for the same layout concern (minWidth: 0)
findings_details.body: Line 220 uses `style={{ minWidth: 0 }}` on the outer flex row while the same file uses Tailwind's `min-w-0` on other elements (e.g. line 76 in TerminalPane). `min-w-0` and `style={{ minWidth: 0 }}` are semantically identical but the inconsistency is a DRY violation — a future Tailwind upgrade that renames `min-w-0` would miss this element. Fix: replace `style={{ minWidth: 0 }}` on line 220 with the `min-w-0` Tailwind class.

findings_details.title: MobileKeyBar is rendered unconditionally for any running session — not just on mobile
findings_details.body: Line 54: `{running && <MobileKeyBar onKey={sendInput} />}`. MobileKeyBar hides itself via `md:hidden` on its root div (MobileKeyBar.tsx line 77), so on desktop it is invisible. However, the component is still mounted, still registers state (the `ctrl` boolean), and still renders 10 buttons into the DOM on every running session on desktop. The `md:hidden` guard exists on the component, but the caller never guards the mount. This adds unnecessary DOM nodes and React state on desktop. For pure-CSS `display:none` this is a minor concern, but it is inconsistent with the project rule 'Mobile-only UI must be `md:hidden` so desktop is byte-identical'. Byte-identical is violated here — the DOM differs. Fix: the `md:hidden` on the component root is the correct place (it keeps the calling site clean), but document the trade-off with a comment or remove the 'byte-identical' aspiration from the review checklist. Alternatively, if truly byte-identical DOM is required, hoist the guard: `{running && isMobile && <MobileKeyBar … />}` where `isMobile` is derived from a `useMediaQuery('(max-width: 767px)')` hook.

findings_details.title: Ctrl+non-letter clears Ctrl state silently without emitting anything — no user feedback
findings_details.body: In the `press` callback (lines 57-64): when `ctrl` is true and the user taps a key that is NOT in CTRL_LETTER_BYTES (e.g. 'Up', 'Down', 'Esc'), the code falls through to `setCtrl(false)` at line 63 and then continues to emit the key's normal bytes. So Ctrl+Up sends the plain cursor-up sequence `\x1b[A` — not wrong, but arguably unexpected. More importantly, the Ctrl button stays highlighted until the user taps another key (because line 63 only fires if ctrl is true at line 62, and setCtrl is async). Actually re-reading: lines 62-64: if(ctrl) setCtrl(false); then fall through to SIMPLE_BYTES or ARROW_BYTES. The state is queued but the current closure still holds ctrl=true. The emission path uses the already-captured `ctrl` variable (not re-read), so SIMPLE_BYTES/ARROW_BYTES branches run correctly. The ctrl indicator just resets after the render. This is functionally correct but the UX is surprising. This is a low-severity UX note, not a bug. Fix (optional): Add an `aria-live` announcement or a visual flash when Ctrl is silently consumed on a non-composable key, to signal to the user that the Ctrl press was discarded. Or document the intended UX in a code comment.

findings_details.title: makeSessionsMock casts `as any` — same contract-hiding problem as useTerminalPanel.test.ts
findings_details.body: makeSessionsMock at line 23 and makeRunningSessionsMock at line 35 both cast `as any`. This suppresses TypeScript errors for missing fields (needsAttention, switchTo, knownActiveTerminals, attachExisting). The test exercises TerminalPanel via real useTerminalPanel hook code, so the missing fields silently make useAutoReattach a no-op in all tests — the reattach path is never exercised. Fix: same as useTerminalPanel.test.ts — use a typed partial or add the missing fields with sensible stub values so TypeScript enforces the contract.

findings_details.title: Error banner test renders App without ToastProvider wrapper
findings_details.body: The test 'renders the connectivity banner when /api/branches fails' at line 113 calls `render(<App />)` directly without the ToastProvider wrapper used by all other tests in the same file (via the `renderApp` helper). If App or any child component calls `useToast()`, this will throw a context error in this test. The other tests in the same describe use `renderApp(App)` which wraps in ToastProvider. Fix: change `render(<App />)` on line 113 to `renderApp(App)`.

findings_details.title: `overscroll-behavior: contain` set on html/body/root (line 23) then overridden to `none` on body (line 35)
findings_details.body: The `html, body, #root` rule at lines 17-24 sets `overscroll-behavior: contain`. Then the `body` rule at lines 28-37 sets `overscroll-behavior: none`. The second declaration wins (later specificity for `body`), making the `contain` on lines 17-24 a dead declaration. `none` is also more aggressive — it suppresses cross-axis overscroll on body but not on scroll-children. If `none` is the intended value for body, the `contain` in the combined selector is misleading dead code and should be removed or commented to show the override is intentional. Fix: remove `overscroll-behavior: contain` from the `html, body, #root` block (line 23), leaving only the `overscroll-behavior: none` on the `body` rule. Keep the comment explaining why body needs `none`.

findings_details.title: sendInput dereferences instance.terminal without null guard
findings_details.body: sendInput guards against !instance (line 171) but does not guard against instance.terminal being undefined. The TerminalSession type has terminal as an optional field — it is absent during the window between launch() storing the instance and the WebSocket completing the xterm Terminal attachment. On a slow connection or rapid Ctrl-tap during connection, instance.terminal.input() throws TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'input'). The makeSessionsMock helper in TerminalPanel.test.tsx builds instances without terminal, confirming the field is optional. Fix: add `if (!instance?.terminal) return;` before the .input() call, or widen the guard on line 171 to `if (!instance || !instance.terminal) return;`.
